file.content:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
    <head>
        <meta charset="utf-8" />
        <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
        <title>Merch Card Web Component demo page</title>
        <script>
            window.process = {
                env: {},
            };
        </script>
        <style>
            main {
                display: flex;
                align-items: center;
                gap: 20px;
                flex-direction: column;
                padding-top: 80px;
            }

            main > div {
                display: initial;
            }

            #sidenav {
                display: none;
            }

            @media screen and (min-width: 1440px) {
                #sidenav {
                    flex: 1;
                    justify-content: stretch;
                    padding-top: 8px;
                    display: flex;
                    position: fixed;
                    bottom: 0px;
                    left: 0px;
                    gap: 20px;
                    flex-direction: column;
                    width: 220px;
                }
            }

            #firstcards {
                background: linear-gradient(
                    90deg,
                    rgba(2, 0, 36, 1) 0%,
                    rgba(140, 43, 95, 0.4430147058823529) 35%,
                    rgba(0, 212, 255, 1) 100%
                );

tool_response.stdout:
# Plan: ADW SDLC Smoke Test Validation

## Task Description
Simple smoke test to validate the ADW SDLC workflow end-to-end. This chore creates a minimal verifiable artifact to confirm the full pipeline (plan → implement → test → review → PR) is functioning correctly.

## Relevant Files
- `specs/PROGRESS.md` — create to track progress (does not yet exist)
- `scripts/` — reference for any available validation scripts
- `package.json` — check available npm scripts for validation

## Step by Step Tasks

### 1. Create PROGRESS.md
- Create `specs/PROGRESS.md` with a log entry marking this smoke test as in-progress

### 2. Add a smoke test marker file
- Create `specs/smoke-test.md` with a simple timestamped entry confirming the ADW SDLC pipeline ran successfully

## Acceptance Criteria
- `specs/PROGRESS.md` exists and contains an entry for issue 4ce84e10
- `specs/smoke-test.md` exists with a valid smoke test entry
- No existing files are modified

## Validation Commands
- `ls specs/PROGRESS.md specs/smoke-test.md`

tool_input.content:
# Plan: Add countdown variant to merch-badge for promo urgency ## Task Description Add a `variant="countdown"` to the existing `merch-badge` LitElement web component. When this variant is set, the badge reads a promotion end date (passed via an `end-date` attribute) and renders an urgency message based on how much time remains. If the promotion has expired, is more than 7 days away, or has no valid end date, the badge renders nothing. The implementation must follow existing badge patterns, use no new dependencies, and support localization via data attributes. ## Relevant Files - `web-components/src/merch-badge.js` — **edit**: add `endDate` property, countdown computation helper, and conditional render logic for `variant="countdown"` - `web-components/src/hydrate.js` — **edit**: in `processBadge` and `processTrialBadge`, when `fields.variant === 'countdown'`, pass `end-date` attribute from promotion data when constructing the `merch-badge` HTML string - `web-components/src/price/utilities.js` — **read-only**: reuse `isPromotionActive` logic pattern; no changes needed - `web-components/test/merch-badge.test.js` — **create**: unit tests for countdown formatting logic and render edge cases ## Step by Step Tasks ### 1. Add `endDate` property to `MerchBadge` - In `merch-badge.js`, add `endDate: { type: String, attribute: 'end-date' }` to `static properties` - Initialize `this.endDate = ''` in `constructor()` ### 2. Add countdown text computation helper - Add a module-level function `getCountdownText(endDateStr, labels)` that: - Returns `null` if `endDateStr` is falsy or unparseable - Parses `endDateStr` via `new Date(endDateStr)`; returns `null` if invalid (`isNaN`) - Computes `msRemaining = endDate - Date.now()` - Returns `null` if `msRemaining <= 0` (expired) - Computes `daysRemaining = Math.ceil(msRemaining / 86400000)` - Returns `null` if `daysRemaining > 7` (too far ahead) - Returns `labels.today` if `daysRemaining < 1` (less than 24 hours, i.e. `msRemaining < 86400000`) - Returns `labels.tomorrow` if `daysRemaining === 1` - Returns `labels.days.replace('{n}', daysRemaining)` for 2–7 days - The `labels` object defaults: `{ today: 'Offer ends today', tomorrow: 'Offer ends tomorrow', days: 'Offer ends in {n} days' }` - Accept override labels from dataset attributes: `data-label-today`, `data-label-tomorrow`, `data-label-days` on the host element (read in `render()` via `this.dataset`) ### 3. Update `render()` in `MerchBadge` for countdown variant - At the top of `render()`, when `this.variant === 'countdown'`: - Build `labels` from `this.dataset` overrides merged over defaults - Call `getCountdownText(this.endDate, labels)` - If result is `null`, `return nothing` (import `nothing` is already imported) - Otherwise render: `html\`
${renderIcon(this.icon)}${countdownText}
\`` - All other variants fall through to the existing `render()` path unchanged ### 4. Add default orange color scheme for countdown in `connectedCallback` - After the existing style property setup in `connectedCallback()`, add a block: ```js if (this.variant === 'countdown') { if (!this.backgroundColor) { this.style.setProperty('--merch-badge-background-color', 'var(--spectrum-orange-500, #E68619)'); } if (!this.color) { this.style.setProperty('--merch-badge-color', '#fff'); } if (!this.icon) { this.icon = 'sp-icon-clock'; } } ``` - This mirrors how existing variants handle defaults without introducing new branches elsewhere ### 5. Update `hydrate.js` `processBadge` to pass `end-date` for countdown - In `processBadge`, inside the block where `fields.badge` is plain text (not already `` string - `fields.badgeEndDate` is sourced from the AEM fragment field (same pipeline that populates `fields.badge`); no new hydration logic is needed beyond reading the field ### 6. Create unit tests - Create `web-components/test/merch-badge.test.js` - Test `getCountdownText` directly (export it for testing) with scenarios: - `null` / undefined endDate → returns null - Invalid date string → returns null - Expired date (past) → returns null - 8+ days out → returns null - 7 days → returns "Offer ends in 7 days" - 2 days → returns "Offer ends in 2 days" - 1 day → returns "Offer ends tomorrow" - < 24 hours → returns "Offer ends today" - Custom labels via overrides - Validate `node --check` passes on the file ## Acceptance Criteria - `variant="countdown"` renders the correct urgency text for 1–7 days remaining - Badge renders nothing for expired, >7 days, or missing/invalid `end-date` - Default orange background and clock icon apply when no explicit color/icon is set - All other badge variants are unaffected - Countdown text is overridable via `data-label-today`, `data-label-tomorrow`, `data-label-days` attributes - No new npm dependencies are introduced - `node --check web-components/src/merch-badge.js` passes ## Validation Commands - `node --check web-components/src/merch-badge.js` - `node --check web-components/src/hydrate.js` - `node --check web-components/test/merch-badge.test.js` - `cd web-components && npm test -- --grep "merch-badge"` (if test runner available)
